seoTitle: Dean vs Deans Marsh — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Dean vs Deans Marsh: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dean and Deans Marsh.
heading: Dean vs Deans Marsh — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dean, Victoria and Deans Marsh, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dean has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Deans Marsh (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dean or Deans Marsh safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dean has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Deans Marsh scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dean scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Deans Marsh scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dean or Deans Marsh?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dean has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.78% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
